name: ccpa
description: CCPA / CPRA rules for code paths handling California consumer personal information (PI). Layer 2 of the four-layer rubric composition per ADR-079.

severity_rules.pattern: Code collects California consumer PI (name, address, geolocation, biometric, browsing history) without a documented disclosure or notice-at-collection reference.
severity_rules.description: CCPA 1798.100(b) requires notice-at-collection disclosing categories of PI collected and purpose.
severity_rules.remediation: Document the categories collected and purpose; surface a notice link adjacent to every collection point.

severity_rules.pattern: Code path that sells or shares PI with third parties has no `Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information` toggle wired to the data flow.
severity_rules.description: CCPA 1798.120 / 1798.135 require a clearly accessible opt-out for sale or sharing of PI.
severity_rules.remediation: Implement an opt-out endpoint and surface a `Do Not Sell` link; gate every PI sale/share path on the opt-out state.

severity_rules.pattern: Project storing California consumer PI has no deletion-request handler that propagates deletion across data stores.
severity_rules.description: CCPA 1798.105 grants consumers a right to deletion that must propagate to every system containing the PI.
severity_rules.remediation: Implement a deletion handler that fans out to caches, replicas, search indexes, and warehouses; emit a confirmation log.

severity_rules.pattern: Application UI or marketing site lacks a footer or header link to the privacy policy describing CCPA rights.
severity_rules.description: CCPA 1798.130 requires that the privacy policy disclosing CCPA rights be accessible from every page that collects PI.
severity_rules.remediation: Add a privacy-policy link to the global footer; ensure the policy enumerates CCPA rights and contact info.

severity_rules.pattern: Outbound integration with a third-party processor receives PI without a documented service-provider contract reference.
severity_rules.description: CCPA 1798.140 service-provider exemption requires a written contract restricting use of PI to the contracted purpose.
severity_rules.remediation: Document the service-provider contract reference at the integration boundary; deny PI to vendors without a signed contract.

severity_rules.pattern: Code offers a discount, loyalty perk, or premium feature in exchange for PI without a financial-incentive notice and opt-in flow.
severity_rules.description: CCPA 1798.125(b) requires a financial-incentive notice and explicit opt-in when value is exchanged for PI.
severity_rules.remediation: Surface a financial-incentive notice describing the value of PI; require explicit opt-in before granting the perk.
